by_judge.judge: D K SINGH & RAJESH RAI K
PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 01.06.2026.

narrative:
On June 2, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 56.3%, resolving or hearing 1,367 out of 2,429 scheduled cases across 45 judges. This represents an increase in both roster volume and efficiency compared to the previous day, June 1, 2026, which recorded 1,115 cases heard out of 2,133 scheduled, yielding a 52.3% hearing rate.

S. Vishwajith Shetty managed the court's highest individual load, hearing 141 out of 260 scheduled matters (54.2%). Other high-volume performers included Suraj Govindaraj, who converted 88 of 107 scheduled cases (82.2%) in Hall 10, and H.P. Sandesh, who addressed 81 of 97 matters (83.5%) in Hall 8. Conversely, B.M. Shyam Prasad saw mixed progress, addressing only 21.0% of his primary 100-case roster, though converting over 92% of his smaller listings.

In terms of procedural stages, Preliminary Hearings achieved the strongest conversion rate at 68.6% (266 of 388), followed by Admissions at 57.0% (251 of 440). Orders, the largest stage by volume with 805 scheduled cases, recorded a 53.2% conversion rate, which is a decline from the 59.1% rate achieved the previous day. Final Hearings remained the slowest category, converting 42.3% of its 404 scheduled matters.

Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the caseload with 998 scheduled cases, maintaining a conversion rate of 55.2% (551 heard) that identically matched the prior day’s performance. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) followed with 328 scheduled and 171 heard (52.1%), while Regular Second Appeals (RSA) demonstrated a high clearance rate of 72.3%, resolving 102 out of 141 scheduled matters.
